visualGoal: Make a frustrated simmer with a dead throttle understand the fix in one glance: free glue software (AntiMicroX) plus a deadzone. This is a low-product-density software how-to — lean on a workflow infographic and a decision-tree graphic, NOT forced product cards. Only one verified product photo is used (the hero), because the tools are software with no product shot.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic marine sim desk at cool teal workshop light: a repurposed flight throttle lever mounted as a ship's engine control in the foreground with an empty product-overlay slot for a real throttle photo, a second monitor faintly showing a sailing sim, a laptop beside it running a generic axis-mapping utility (no real UI, no fake logos), the message being 'software brings the dead lever to life', 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake software UI, real app screenshots, fake logos, warped hardware, unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Which mapping tool do you need?
content: Lots of buttons / you own it -> JoyToKey ($7.50)
Game grabs wrong device -> vJoy decoy fed by JoyToKey (free)
Free tools frustrate you -> reWASD (Lifetime from ~EUR34.99)
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center decision tree, large readable text, marine teal/gold, clear branch arrows

infographicPrompts.title: Axis-to-key in 5 steps
content: 1 Turn the game's Controller setting OFF
2 Read the sim's real throttle/rudder keys
3 Map axis low-half + high-half to those keys
4 Set a center DEADZONE (stops runaway input)
5 Save a per-game profile and back it up
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered horizontal workflow ladder, deadzone step highlighted as the critical one, marine palette

infographicPrompts.title: Stepped keys vs true analog
content: No software can invent analog the engine never reads
infographicPrompts.style: Two-lane comparison graphic (staircase vs smooth ramp), honest and plain, marine palette

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine-sim curator Gus Calder at a tidy home helm desk, one hand resting on a repurposed throttle lever, a laptop beside him showing a generic mapping utility (no real UI), calm practical-tinkerer energy, product-overlay space beside him, 4:5 portrait, cool teal workshop light.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.note: If an app-screenshot is desired, it must be a genuine AntiMicroX capture we own/are licensed to use — do NOT fabricate a fake UI. Prefer the axis-to-key workflow infographic instead. Optional, low priority.

openQuestions: Do we want a genuine (owned/licensed) AntiMicroX screenshot, or is the workflow infographic sufficient? Infographic preferred to avoid any fake-UI risk.

Should a proper editorial bay hero replace the interim verified gear-photo hero? Per brief, heroImage had to be a verified path (all verified paths are /images/gear/...), so a throttle photo is the interim hero; a mapping-workflow composite would be a stronger bay hero if produced.
Confirm live USD price for reWASD Lifetime before any graphic quotes a hard dollar figure (article uses EUR34.99 / ~$35+).
Is a SEPARATE ECDIS/charts software explainer wanted (the brief's alternate angle)? That is a new article, not part of this manifest.
